tools.name: list_files
tools.description:
List all files in the specified directory.

Args:
    path: Optional subdirectory path relative to the base directory

tools.name: read_file
tools.description:
Read the contents of a file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the file relative to the base directory

tools.name: write_file
tools.description:
Write content to a file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the file relative to the base directory
    content: Content to write to the file

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description:
Delete a file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the file relative to the base directory

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'list_files' claims unrestricted access scope
findings.description: Tool 'list_files' description contains a broad-scope claim: 'all files'. Unrestricted scope tools risk being exploited for lateral movement or privilege escalation in an MCP session.
findings.evidence:
List all files in the specified directory.

Args:
    path: Optional subdirectory path relative to the base directory
findings.remediation: Scope tools to the minimum necessary access. Replace 'any path' / 'all files' with explicit allowlists. Validate and restrict inputs server-side.

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'write_file' accepts write-path param (path traversal risk)
findings.description: Tool 'write_file' accepts a file path parameter (file_path) and its description indicates write operations. Without path sanitization, callers may supply traversal sequences to write files outside the intended directory.
findings.evidence:
path param(s): file_path | description: Write content to a file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the file relative to the base directory
    content: Content to write to the file
findings.remediation: Validate and canonicalize file path inputs. Restrict writes to an explicit base directory using path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() checks. Manual verification recommended.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'read_file' conflicts with a high-value built-in name
findings.description: Tool 'read_file' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'write_file' conflicts with a high-value built-in name
findings.description: Tool 'write_file' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'delete_file' conflicts with a high-value built-in name
findings.description: Tool 'delete_file' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: 'list_files' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'read_file.file_path' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'read_file' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'write_file.file_path' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'write_file' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'delete_file.file_path' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'delete_file' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: initialize accepts type-confused protocolVersion (integer instead of string)
findings.description: The server accepted an initialize request where protocolVersion was an integer (42) instead of a string. Strict type checking prevents parser confusion and unexpected coercion behavior.
findings.remediation: Validate that protocolVersion is a string before processing.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 4 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
